steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what tracking citation frequency lets you do with a scholar's importance. The passage gives an example: Mester is frequently cited, which shows her work is significant. So the blank needs a word meaning to assess or gauge importance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "diminish": Diminish means to reduce or make smaller. Plugging in: tracking citations would reduce a scholar's importance. But the passage says frequent citations indicate significance — citing someone more doesn't shrink their importance. ✗
  • "increase": Increase means to make larger. Plugging in: tracking citations would grow a scholar's importance. Tracking only reveals existing importance; it doesn't create or add to it. ✗
  • "vary": Vary means to change or differ. Plugging in: tracking citations would alter a scholar's importance. But citation tracking measures a fixed quality — it doesn't change that quality. ✗
  • "measure": Measure means to assess the extent of something. Plugging in: tracking how often others cite a scholar is one way to measure how important that scholar's research is. This matches exactly — the Mester example is the measuring instrument. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
